meta.source: Decompiled C# source code from World of Sea Battle (Common.dll)
notes: All formulas extracted from decompiled ILSpy output
Some variable names are obfuscated (_007BXXXX_007D pattern) — reconstructed from context
MSpeed values in the bonus system are internal units; display speed = internal / 2 for some contexts
Speed constant CShipMovement = 0.00036421 converts internal speed to world units per second

damageCalculation.description: How damage is applied to a ship's HP. Damage flows through MakeDamage() which applies HP damage, sail damage, and various critical effects.

variables.DamageModifier: 1.0 normally; /1.5 for player on education map; *1.2 for PvP on education map
variables.DamageReduce: Min(0.99, GetBonus(PReduceDamage)) — applied server-side via ReduceDamageForServer property

notes: Damage cannot be negative (throws ArgumentException)
If ship is already destroyed, damage is ignored
On education maps: player takes damage/1.5, PvP damage is *1.2
Marching mode speed reduced on damage: marchModeBonusSpeed -= HealthDamage/(1+currentHP)*100 + SailDamage*100
Flooding factor > 0 stops flooding on powder keg, mortar, burning, stern damage, or fireworks hits
Collision damage can reduce flooding by HealthDamage/MaxHp

hitLocationMechanics.source: Ship.cs MakeDamage + MakeCollisionDamage, corrected with in-game wiki

bow.description: Bow (nose) takes REDUCED damage compared to broadside hits
bow.effect: PReduceDamageToNose — built-in damage reduction for bow hits
bow.note: This is a base game mechanic, not just a player bonus

stern.description: Stern hits while flooding cause INSTANT SINK
stern.note: StopFloodingSynchronized does NOT cancel flooding — it finalizes it and sinks the ship

instantSinkWhileFlooding.description: The following damage types while a ship is flooding cause instant destruction
instantSinkWhileFlooding.code: if (PowderKeg || MortarShot || (Burning && burningDuration > 0) || SternDamage || Fireworks) → StopFloodingSynchronized() → INSTANT SINK

sails.description: Each sail has individual HP. Sail damage reduces speed proportional to sail weight.

broadside.description: Standard hull damage — no special modifiers. This is the default hit zone.

confirmedFormula.formula: Damage = max(0, Penetration - EffectiveArmor) × Ammo.DamageFactor × BonusEffects

effectiveArmor.description: Armor thickness increases at an angle, reducing damage
effectiveArmor.broadside: EffectiveArmor = BaseArmor (flat angle = easiest to penetrate)
effectiveArmor.bow: EffectiveArmor is HIGHEST at bow (more sturdy built-in)
effectiveArmor.stern: EffectiveArmor is LOWEST at stern (less armored)
effectiveArmor.note: Exact angle multiplier formula is server-side, not in client code

confirmedFormula.example: Cannon pen=10, armor=2 → broadside damage = (10-2) × ammo_factor = 8 × ammo_factor
confirmedFormula.zeroDamage: If Penetration <= EffectiveArmor, damage is 0 (cannon cannot penetrate)
confirmedFormula.ammoFactor: Each ammo type has DamageFactor multiplier (round shot = 1.0)
confirmedFormula.tooltipSource: Armor - the thickness of the side cloth, which reduces damage from cannonballs. If the penetration of a cannon is 10 and the armor is 2, then the ship will take 8 points of damage * ammo type damage and effects. At an angle, the thickness increases, which reduces damage. The bow is more sturdy and the stern is less armored

speedCalculation.description: Ship speed is a combination of base speed, percentage bonuses, flat bonuses, wind effects, paddles, cargo, and marching mode.

variables.PSpeed: Percentage speed bonus from upgrades, crew, design elements, dynamic effects, food

speedCalculation.effectiveSpeedFormula: currentMaxSpeed = Max(3, Speed * bigHoleFactor * SailSpeedFactor) + StormBonusSpeed

windEffect.description: Wind affects effective max speed based on ship orientation relative to wind
windEffect.formula: effectiveMaxSpeed = Max(Min(maxSpeed, 2), maxSpeed * (windEffectivity * 0.85 + 0.15)) when going forward
windEffect.backwindFormula: effectiveMaxSpeed = Max(Max(maxSpeed, -CBackwindSpeed(baseSpeed)), maxSpeed * ((1-windDot)*0.8 + 0.2)) when going backward
notes: windDot is dot product of ship+wind blended normal with wind direction
Ships with HasUniqueWindRose get special windDot capping at 0.95+
Rivers override low wind effectivity: effectivity = Max(riverLevel * 0.5, effectivity)
Wind also causes slight yaw drift: -sign(tiltX) * 0.004 rad/sec
Ships without steam wheel or paddles get additional wind yaw: up to 0.19 * 0.25 rad/sec

speedAcceleration.description: Speed doesn't change instantly — acceleration depends on crew, inertia, and current vs target speed
speedAcceleration.acceleratingFormula: speedChange = crewFactor * targetFactor * sailAnimFactor * dt * (1 - driftAmount)

mobilityCalculation.description: Mobility determines turning rate. Affected by HP, sail HP, bonuses, and drift.
mobilityCalculation.formula: Mobility = (BasicMobility + GetBonus(MMobilityBonus) + GetBonus(PMobilityBonus) * BasicMobility + lowHpBonus) * sailDamageFactor

driftMechanics.description: At high speeds, turning causes drift which slows the ship and reduces mobility

armorCalculation.description: Armor reduces incoming damage. Calculated from base + bonuses.

healthCalculation.description: Max HP from base + bonuses, with integrity penalty for destroyed integrity.

variables.integrityFactor: 0.5 if integrity is destroyed (Integrity==0 && MaxIntegrity!=-1), else 1.0

capacityCalculation.description: Cargo capacity determines how much weight the ship can carry.
notes: Bonuses exclude temporary/dynamic effects (_007B2824_007D: false)
FreeCapacity = Max(0, Capacity - GetItemsMass())
GetItemsMass() = ballsOfHold.ComputeMass + PrivateResourcesOfHold.ComputeMass + powderKegsOfHold.ComputeMass

poundageMap.description: Maps ship rank to mortar poundage — higher rank ships use smaller (lower poundage) mortars with different stats

damage.description: Mortar damage is based on penetration stat which scales by poundage/rank, plus splash damage falloff
damage.penetration: CannonGameInfo.PenetrationMortar(shipInfo) — looked up by rank via poundage map
damage.sailDamageFromMortar: Max(0, 1 - GetBonus(PSailProtection)) — targets can reduce sail damage

reload.description: Mortar reload time scales by ship rank via poundage map
reload.formula: ReloadTimeMortar(shipInfo) — milliseconds, looked up from XML by rank

distance.description: Mortar min/max distance scales by rank
distance.formula: DistanceMortar(shipInfo) returns (minDist, maxDist) tuple per rank
distance.distanceBonus: GetBonus(MMortarMaxDistance) — flat bonus to max distance

trajectory.description: Mortar projectile follows a parabolic arc

preparationTime.needsPreparation: Only non-Default feature mortars need preparation

boardingMechanics.description: Boarding involves crew combat with damage, health, and capacity mechanics

crewDamageFromCannons.minimumCrew: If crew would drop to 1 or below, no damage applied

boardingWeight.summaryCapacity: Sum of all units' Capacity stat

crewCombatStats.summaryDamagePerSec: Sum of (unit.SampleDamage / (BoardingUpdateIntervalMs/1000)) * count for each unit type
crewCombatStats.summaryHealth: Sum of unit.Health * count for each unit type

boardingMechanics.goldBonus: GetBonus(PBoardingGold) — percentage bonus to gold from boarding

reload.description: Cannon reload time from base stat with bonuses
reload.singleShotBonus: GetBonus(PReloadingAfterSingleShot) — extra reload speed after a single-shot volley
reload.premiumBonus: 0.4 (40%) for 3rd slot reload boost with premium
reload.crewEffectivityFormula: ratio = Min(1, sailors/needSailors); result = ratio + ratio*(1-ratio)/2 * 0.75

penetration.description: Penetration value determines how much armor the cannonball can pierce

range.distanceBonus: Min(15, GetBonus(MCannonsDistance)) — flat distance bonus, capped at 15

accuracy.worseScatter: DoubleShot and TripleShot features have worse scatter

satietyBonuses.description: When crew is fed (satiety/food bonus > 0), these bonuses are applied proportionally

bonuses.description: Speed change bonus +5% at full satiety

crewEffectivity.formula: ratio = Min(1, sailors / needSailors); effectivity = ratio + ratio*(1-ratio)/2 * 0.75

specialUnits.conflicts: Pirates conflict with Combats — conflicting units get 50% effectiveness
specialUnits.endConflicts: BSpecialUnitEndConflicts bonus removes conflict penalty and adds +1 slot

unitTypes.sailor: Affects ship speed and reload via effectivity ratio
unitTypes.boarding: Combat units for boarding — contribute damage, health, capacity
unitTypes.special: Unique units with special bonuses, have contract time and salary

wind.description: Wind direction affects ship speed and handling
wind.windDotCalculation: Dot of (shipNormal + windNormal * blendFactor).Normalized with windNormal
wind.yawDrift: Wind pushes ship yaw by -sign(tiltX) * 0.004 rad/sec
wind.additionalYawDrift: Without steam/paddles: up to -sign(tiltX) * Min(0.19, Pow(Max(0, (1-effectivity-0.15)/0.85), 1.7)) * 0.25 * (1-abs(axisInertion))
wind.sailRotation: Sails visually rotate to match wind angle

storm.description: Storm/rain affects ship tilt, speed, and handling

destructionTilt.condition: RainingLevel > 0.5 AND not destroyed/flooding AND not education map
destructionTilt.effect: Ship tilts and sinks, reducing effective height by destructByTilt * CorpusHalfHeight * 0.5

notes: Fog mechanics are primarily visual/client-side, weather engine handles fog rendering

speedCaps.description: Various speed limits and constraints in the game
speedCaps.minimumSpeed: Max(3, ...) — ship always has at least 3 effective max speed
speedCaps.balloonDestroyedSpeed: 0 when balloon is destroyed or flooding
speedCaps.shallowWaterPenalty: Max(0.0001, 1 - shallowWaterLevel) — reduces speed near shallow areas
speedCaps.driftSpeedThreshold: 18 knots (19 for rank 2+) — above this, turning causes drift
speedCaps.lowHpMarchingDebuff: Saturate((0.25 - hpFactor) / 0.15) * 0.33 — reduces marching speed gain at low HP

cargoSpeedPenalty.description: Carrying cargo reduces effective max speed. The penalty is proportional to how much cargo you carry relative to capacity.
cargoSpeedPenalty.overloadVisualEffect: Ship sinks lower by Saturate(1-capacitySpeedFactor) * CorpusHalfHeight * 0.27
notes: Ship ID 1 always has factor 1.0 (likely starter/tutorial ship)
basicCapacitySpeedFactor is initialized to 1.0 on ship creation
SpeedWithoutTempEffects = BasicSpeed * (1 + PSpeed_noTemp) + MSpeed_noTemp

marchingMode.description: Full sails mode (speed 3) gradually builds up bonus speed over time
marchingMode.activationCondition: LinearStateCode == 3 AND AxisStateCode == 0 AND NowSpeed >= PrevMaxSpeed - 0.3 AND not destroyed/flooding
marchingMode.effectOnDisplaySpeed: PrevDisplayMaxSpeedIncMarchMode = PrevDisplayMaxSpeed + MaxExtraMarchSpeed * Saturate(1.33 * SailSpeedFactor * CapacitySpeedFactor)
marchingMode.ship38Debuff: If ship ID 38 and in battle: speed -= 2.5 / PatSpeed

integritySystem.description: Capital ships have an integrity system that degrades over time and requires repair

restoreDuration.specialShips: 25 * amount seconds for ship IDs 62 and 65

restoreCost.description: Cost scales with damage fraction and ship rank/type
restoreCost.resourcePortion: 0.125 (with premium at certain port) or 0.25 of costMultiplier
restoreCost.empireBadges: Max(maxIntegrity - currentIntegrity, existing badge cost) for empire rank <=2

bonusCalculation.description: Ship bonuses come from 5 sources: Upgrades, Design Elements, Crew, Powerup Items, and Dynamic (temporary) Effects + Food bonus
bonusCalculation.standardFormula: totalBonus = Upgrades.Effects[effect] + ExtraUpgradeFine + DesignElements[effect] + Crew.Effects[effect] + PowerupItems[effect] + DynamicBonus + FoodBonus

extraUpgradeFine.description: Penalty for using the last upgrade slot
extraUpgradeFine.condition: Applied when the MaxUpgradesCount-1 slot has an upgrade installed

multiplicativeBonuses.description: Some bonuses use multiplicative stacking instead of additive

additiveBonuses.description: Most bonuses stack additively from all sources
additiveBonuses.allOtherEffects: Sum of all source values for the effect

dynamicEffects.description: Temporary effects with timeouts, and permanent arena upgrades
dynamicEffects.temporaryDuration: TimeoutMs in milliseconds, decremented each frame
dynamicEffects.duplicateHandling: Same ByPowerupItemId + same Effect = extend timeout to max of existing and new

upgradeWear.description: Upgrades degrade over time based on usage
upgradeWear.wornOutEffect: Upgrade effects not applied when Strength <= 0

costGoldStrength.costFactor: Rank>=5: 1.3, Rank>=2: 1.8, else 2.5; overridden to 1.8 if playerRank>17, 2.2 if rank>25 & shipRank>=5

sailMechanics.sailHPCalculation: Weighted average of individual sail hitbox strengths

sailRestore.formula: Each damaged sail: strength += restoreAmount / (damagedSailCount * sailWeight)
sailRestore.description: Restoration is split evenly among damaged sails, weighted by sail weight

collisionMechanics.cooldownRecovery: cooldown += 0.4 per debuff, max 5, decays by dt

kegPositions.2kegs: Spread 0.4 rad apart behind, extra radius +1
kegPositions.3kegs: Spread 0.6 rad apart behind, extra radius +3
kegPositions.4kegs: Two pairs: inner 0.3 rad spread (dist+5), outer 0.7 rad spread (dist-1), extra radius +2.5

paddleMechanics.description: Ships with paddles or steam wheels get bonus speed independent of wind

paddleSpeed.crewEffectivity: * Crew.Effectivity (for paddle ships only, not steam wheel)

paddleMechanics.windInteraction: When paddle speed > 3 and wind is against (windDot < 0), sails are closed

shipwayDuration.description: Time to build a ship at the shipway, by rank

completeEffectEnum.description: All ShipBonusEffect values — P = percentage, M = flat, B = boolean

BMoreFireAreaAndBurning.description: Boolean: more fire area and burning

PSpecialUnitImperialUniqueChance.description: Percentage chance for imperial special unit

ServMendingStrengthTemporal.description: Server-side temporal strength mending per sec

BDestroyFloodingShopByCollision.description: Boolean: destroy flooding ship by collision

PXpBonusUpgradesAndBranch.description: Percentage XP bonus from upgrades and branch

PSpecialUnitGameModeUniqueChance.description: Percentage special unit game mode chance

PReloadingAfterSingleShot.description: Percentage reload bonus after single shot

PCannonsReloadWithLowHp.description: Percentage cannon reload bonus at low HP

BSpecialUnitEndConflicts.description: Boolean: special unit ends crew conflicts

PMortarReloadWhenCannonsAreReloaded.description: Percentage mortar reload when cannons ready

PDecreaseSpeedLossMarchingMode.description: Percentage decrease speed loss in marching

MMobility0And1.description: Flat mobility bonus at low speeds (divided by 100)

BAllowConsumeAnimalsToFood.description: Boolean: allow consuming animals as food

PSpecialUnitChanseAtSea.description: Percentage special unit chance at sea

PRestoreHealthWhenGettingDebris.description: Percentage HP restore when collecting debris

BBoardingStartsWithNoTarget.description: Boolean: boarding starts with no target

BExtraItemHasNoTimeout.description: Boolean: extra item has no timeout

BCrewWillntDeadInSea.description: Boolean: crew won't die in sea

BAllowPowderKegAnySpeed.description: Boolean: allow powder kegs at any speed

PMoreRareItemsAtSea.description: Percentage more rare items at sea

PPowderKegIncreaseTriggerRadius.description: Percentage powder keg trigger radius increase

MPReduceCannonsQuantity.description: Flat reduce cannons quantity in boarding

angleOfImpactDamage.what: How angle of cannonball impact affects damage through armor
angleOfImpactDamage.known: In-game wiki says flat angle = more effective penetration, bow is harder to penetrate
angleOfImpactDamage.missing: Exact formula for angle multiplier on damage/armor
angleOfImpactDamage.testPlan: Fire same cannon at bow vs broadside vs stern of same ship at same range, compare damage numbers

armorDamageReduction.what: Exact formula for damage = penetration - armor and how armor scales
armorDamageReduction.known: Wiki confirms damage = penetration - defense. Low pen cannons fail vs high armor + bow
armorDamageReduction.missing: Is it strictly (penetration - armor) or is there a multiplier/curve? Does distance affect it?
armorDamageReduction.testPlan: Use different penetration cannons against ships with known armor values, record damage

bowDamageReduction.what: Exact bow damage reduction without Iron Ram upgrade
bowDamageReduction.known: In-game wiki says bow is stronger. Iron Ram adds 20%. Bow figureheads add 3-5%.
bowDamageReduction.missing: Is there inherent bow reduction or is it purely from angle-of-impact on armor?
bowDamageReduction.testPlan: Test ship with no bow upgrades - fire at bow vs broadside with same cannon, compare damage
